zh.lifeSummary: 當John Cummings於1540年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾時，他的父親Thomas Cumming of Altyre37歲，母親Margaret Gordon38歲。他於1559年6月20日與Elizabeth Rayner在英國戴文比曉普斯 尼普頓結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1610年3月2日在英國戴文莫爾哈德 畢曉普享年70歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾福利斯。

nameMeanings.name: Cummings
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): variant of Cumming , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Cumings
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Cummings  .

variants.name: Comans
meanings: Dutch: variant of Coomans, Coopmans and Koopmans , a genitivized occupational name from koopman ‘merchant’.

variants.name: Commons
meanings: English and Irish (Mayo, Tipperary, and Galway): variant of   Cummings  .
English: variant of Common , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Cummins
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Cummings  .

variants.name: Cumming
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

variants.name: Comings
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Cummings  , of Norman origin. This surname is now extinct in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When John Cummings was born in 1540, in Elginshire, Scotland, his father, Thomas Cumming of Altyre, was 37 and his mother, Margaret Gordon, was 38. He married Elizabeth Rayner on 20 June 1559, in Bishops Nympton, Devon, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 2 March 1610, in Morchard Bishop, Devon, England, at the age of 70, and was buried in Forres, Elginshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Cummings est né en 1540, à Elginshire, Écosse, son père, Thomas Cumming of Altyre, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Margaret Gordon, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Rayner le 20 juin 1559, à Bishops Nympton, Devon, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 2 mars 1610, à Morchard Bishop, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Forres, Elginshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Cummings 1540 in Elginshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Cumming of Altyre 37 und seine Mutter Margaret Gordon 38. Er hat am 20. Juni 1559 in Bishops Nympton, Devon, England Elizabeth Rayner geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 2. März 1610 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Morchard Bishop, Devon, England und wurde in Forres, Elginshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Cummings è nato il nel 1540 nel Elginshire, Scotland, il suo padre, Thomas Cumming of Altyre, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Margaret Gordon, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Rayner il 20 giugno 1559, nel Bishops Nympton, Devon, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 2 marzo 1610, nel Morchard Bishop, Devon, England, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Forres, Elginshire, Scotland.

ko.lifeSummary: John Cummings(이)가 1540년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Cumming of Altyre(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Gordon(은)는 38세였다. 그는 1559년 6월 20일 영국 데폰 비숍스 니프턴에서 Elizabeth Rayner(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1610년 3월 2일 영국 데폰 모르처드 비숍에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 엘긴실르 포러스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Cummings nasceu em 1540, em Elginshire, Scotland, seu pai, Thomas Cumming of Altyre, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Gordon, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Rayner em 20 de junho de 1559, em Bishops Nympton, Devon, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 2 de março de 1610, em Morchard Bishop, Devon, Inglaterra, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Forres, Elginshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Cummings родился в 1540 г. в местности элгиншир, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Thomas Cumming of Altyre было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Gordon, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Rayner 20 июня 1559 г. в местности бишопс нимптон, девон, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 2 марта 1610 г. в местности морчард бишоп, девон, Англия в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности форресь, элгиншир, Шотландия.

Murió el 2 de marzo de 1610, en Morchard Bishop, Devon, Inglaterra, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Forres, Elginshire, Scotland.
